Water Dataflows

Water Dataflows
  • Introduction
  • View page source

Introduction

The purpose of this notebook is to instruct the data operator on the operational steps in how to operate a dataflow. Other documentation such as development, database diagrams needs to be placed in other pages so it doesn’t clutter the understanding of the work performed.

We are currently in a design phase where we (together) will try to bring structure into all this.

WISE Helpdesk Support

Helpdesk Portal (OTRS private system): OTRS

  • Internal Helpdesk FAQS

FAQs page for internal user (Taskman project): Wise-helpdesk

Guidelines & rules

  • Identify the tickets clearly in the subject once received I.e. [CountryCode - ReportingObligationTag- Subject of issue]

  • Expected Time Of Service is about 1-3 days (first analysis and manual reply)

  • For tickets opened by us in order to get feedback from MS - Set ticket to ‘Pending reminder’ and send 2 reminders before closing

Procedures

  • How to add a new reporter to a EIONET role (example):

Sometimes countries may ask to add someone to the list of reporters. In this case, we’ll just have to follow these guidelines: https://helpdesk.eionet.europa.eu/otrs/index.pl?Action=AgentFAQZoom;ItemID=39;Nav

  • Roles for WISE-SoE The WISE SoE helpdesk doesn’t have permissions to add new national reporters to the respective lists. Please follow the instructions provided in the WISE SoE 2020 data call announcement (https://www.eionet.europa.eu/etcs/etc-icm/etc-news/etc-icm-data-call-wise-soe-data-call-2020):

“Please contact your EIONET NFP if you require reporting permissions on a given dataflow, so that your user name is added to the authorised reporter list. Note that the WISE-4 reporters must be authorised as WISE-6 reporters to be able to report the water quality data, if it has not already been done.”
